test_cases.description: resolve_output_format returns 'text' unchanged even when is_tty is False, ensuring explicit format is never overridden by TTY detection
resolve_output_format returns 'json' unchanged even when is_tty is True, ensuring explicit json format is never overridden
format_output in JSON mode correctly serializes arbitrary response models, not just a hardcoded model type
test_cases.input_description: Multiple different Pydantic response model types (e.g., BlastRadiusResponse, ConflictsResponse, FindingsResponse) serialized to JSON
assertions: Each model's JSON output is valid JSON
Each output matches the respective model's .model_dump(mode='json')

test_cases.description: format_output in text mode produces non-empty strings for all response model types, not just one
test_cases.input_description: Multiple different Pydantic response model types passed with output_format='text'
assertions: Each result is a non-empty string
No trailing newline on any output

test_cases.description: format_output correctly serializes response models with non-empty nested lists (e.g., BlastRadiusResponse with multiple affected_nodes)
test_cases.input_description: A BlastRadiusResponse with 3+ affected_nodes at varying depths
assertions: Parsed JSON contains 'affected_nodes' list with correct length
All nested node objects are present and properly serialized

test_cases.description: format_output never adds trailing newline for any response model type, not just the one tested visibly
test_cases.input_description: Several different response model types formatted as text
assertions: No output string ends with a newline character

test_cases.description: format_error in text mode includes both node_id and field context when both are non-empty simultaneously
format_error text output follows the 'Error [{error_code}]: {message}' pattern as specified in the contract
format_error JSON output contains all CliError fields including node_id and field even when empty
format_error in JSON mode works correctly for various error_code values, not just one hardcoded code
test_cases.input_description: CliError instances with different error_codes like 'registry_not_initialized', 'permission_denied', 'port_conflict', 'empty_review_id'
assertions: Each JSON output has the correct error_code
Each JSON output is valid JSON

test_cases.description: map_exception_to_exit_code returns NOT_FOUND_4 for different not-found exception instances, not just one specific type
test_cases.input_description: Various entity-not-found exceptions (node not found, run not found, file not found)

test_cases.description: map_exception_to_exit_code returns IO_ERROR_3 for various filesystem/network exception types
test_cases.input_description: Different IO exceptions (permission denied, write failure, port in use)

test_cases.description: map_exception_to_exit_code returns USAGE_ERROR_2 for multiple validation exception types
test_cases.input_description: Different validation exceptions (invalid tier, empty tiers, schema violation, invalid JSON, empty review ID)

test_cases.description: map_exception_to_exit_code returns DOMAIN_ERROR_1 for completely unknown exception types like RuntimeError or custom exceptions
test_cases.input_description: RuntimeError('unexpected'), TypeError('bad type'), a custom exception class not in the domain

test_cases.description: NodeIdType accepts node IDs containing dots, dashes, and underscores which are valid per the ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$ pattern
NodeIdType rejects node IDs containing spaces which don't match the allowed pattern
NodeIdType rejects node IDs containing forward slashes which don't match the allowed pattern
NodeIdType rejects node IDs containing @ symbol which doesn't match the allowed pattern
NodeIdType accepts a single valid character as a node ID since the pattern requires 1+ characters
VersionType accepts semver strings with various pre-release suffixes, not just 'alpha.1'
VersionType rejects version strings with leading 'v' prefix like 'v1.2.3'
VersionType rejects version strings with four numeric parts like '1.2.3.4'
VersionType rejects version strings missing the patch number like '1.2'
VersionType accepts '0.0.0' as a valid semver string
RunIdType accepts any valid UUID v4 format string, not just one specific UUID
test_cases.input_description: Multiple different valid UUID v4 strings
assertions: All valid UUID v4s pass validation

test_cases.description: RunIdType rejects UUID strings that are not v4 format
BlastRadiusResponse total_affected always equals the actual length of affected_nodes list for any number of affected nodes
test_cases.input_description: A blast radius computation returning multiple affected nodes

test_cases.description: BlastRadiusResponse max_depth equals the maximum depth among affected_nodes, verified with non-trivial multi-depth results
test_cases.input_description: Blast radius engine returns nodes at depths 1, 2, and 3
assertions: response.max_depth == max(n.depth for n in response.affected_nodes)

test_cases.description: Canary results escape_rate is correctly computed as total_escapes / total_canaries for non-zero canary counts
test_cases.input_description: Canary engine returns 3 escapes out of 10 canaries

test_cases.description: Canary results total_escapes always equals the length of the escapes list
test_cases.input_description: Canary engine returns specific number of escape entries

test_cases.description: FindingsResponse total always equals the actual length of the findings list
test_cases.input_description: Findings engine returns multiple findings for a node

test_cases.description: ConflictsResponse total always equals the actual length of the conflicts list
test_cases.input_description: Conflicts engine returns a mix of resolved and unresolved conflicts

test_cases.description: When unresolved_only is True, no returned conflict should have resolved=True, verified with mixed input data
test_cases.input_description: Conflicts engine has both resolved and unresolved conflicts, command called with unresolved_only=True
assertions: All returned conflicts have resolved == False
No conflict with resolved=True appears in output

test_cases.description: cmd_init response includes a non-empty created_files list containing the files actually created
test_cases.input_description: Normal init with mocked registry.initialize_registry returning file paths

test_cases.description: cmd_trust_show works correctly for different node_id values, not hardcoded to a specific one
test_cases.input_description: Two different node IDs like 'serviceA' and 'data-store.v2' queried sequentially with mocked trust_engine
assertions: Each response contains the correct node_id matching the query

test_cases.description: cmd_trust_reset_taint response includes the exact review_id that was provided in the request
test_cases.input_description: Reset taint with a specific review_id string
assertions: response.review_id matches the input review_id exactly

test_cases.description: cmd_authority_show delegates to authority_engine, never to trust_engine, enforcing trust/authority separation
test_cases.input_description: Mock both trust_engine and authority_engine, invoke authority show

test_cases.description: cmd_soak_compute delegates computation to soak_engine, not to trust_engine or other engines directly
test_cases.input_description: Mock soak_engine, invoke soak compute

test_cases.description: When cmd_register encounters an error, the error message appears on stderr and not on stdout
test_cases.input_description: Invoke register with a nonexistent file, capture stdout and stderr separately
assertions: stdout is empty or contains no error information

test_cases.description: When cmd_blast_radius encounters a node_not_found error, the error appears on stderr not stdout
test_cases.input_description: Invoke blast-radius for a non-existent node, capture output streams
assertions: stdout does not contain error message

test_cases.description: arbiter_group with default config path (./arbiter.yaml) that doesn't exist uses defaults instead of raising config_not_found
test_cases.input_description: Invoke arbiter group without --config flag, when ./arbiter.yaml does not exist

test_cases.description: arbiter_group raises registry_path_invalid when registry_path points to a file rather than a directory
test_cases.input_description: Provide --registry-path pointing to an existing regular file

test_cases.description: cmd_init returns exit code 0 (SUCCESS_0) on successful initialization
test_cases.input_description: Successful init invocation via CliRunner

test_cases.description: cmd_register returns exit code 0 (SUCCESS_0) on successful registration
test_cases.input_description: Successful register invocation via CliRunner with mocked registry

test_cases.description: cmd_trust_show returns exit code 4 (NOT_FOUND_4) when the node doesn't exist
test_cases.input_description: Trust show for a non-existent node via CliRunner

test_cases.description: format_output in JSON mode preserves float values without rounding, matching model_dump exactly
test_cases.input_description: A TrustShowResponse with current_score=0.123456789
assertions: JSON output contains the float value matching model_dump precision

test_cases.description: format_output in JSON mode correctly handles response models with empty lists
test_cases.input_description: FindingsResponse with empty findings list and total=0
assertions: JSON output has 'findings' as empty array

test_cases.description: cmd_canary_inject response run_id is a valid UUID v4 format string
test_cases.input_description: Successful canary inject with mocked canary_engine

test_cases.description: cmd_report response generated_at is a valid UTC ISO 8601 timestamp string
test_cases.input_description: Successful report generation with mocked report_engine
assertions: generated_at can be parsed as ISO 8601

test_cases.description: cmd_watch fails with appropriate error when registry is not initialized
test_cases.input_description: Invoke watch command with mocked registry check that raises registry_not_initialized

test_cases.description: cmd_findings fails when registry is not initialized, not just for node_not_found
test_cases.input_description: Invoke findings with mocked engine that raises registry_not_initialized

test_cases.description: cmd_soak_compute fails when registry is not initialized
test_cases.input_description: Invoke soak compute with mocked engine that raises registry_not_initialized

test_cases.description: cmd_register fails with permission_denied error when the access graph file cannot be read due to permissions
test_cases.input_description: Invoke register with mocked registry that raises permission_denied

test_cases.description: No Python traceback or stack trace leaks to user output when a domain exception occurs in any command
test_cases.input_description: Invoke blast-radius for non-existent node, capture all output

test_cases.description: format_error correctly handles error messages containing special characters like quotes, angle brackets, and newlines
assertions: In JSON mode, output is still valid JSON
In text mode, the message content is preserved

test_cases.description: VersionType accepts semver pre-release identifiers containing multiple dots like '1.0.0-rc.1.2'
VersionType rejects an empty string as a version tag
NodeIdType rejects node IDs containing colons which are not in the allowed character set
cmd_conflicts with unresolved_only=False returns both resolved and unresolved conflicts
test_cases.input_description: Conflicts engine has both resolved=True and resolved=False conflicts, called with unresolved_only=False
assertions: Both resolved and unresolved conflicts appear in response
total matches total count including resolved ones

test_cases.description: cmd_canary_inject with multiple valid tiers returns tiers_covered matching all input tiers
test_cases.input_description: Canary inject with 3 different valid tiers

test_cases.description: cmd_trust_reset_taint fails with ledger_corrupted when trust ledger checksum validation fails
test_cases.input_description: Mock trust_engine.reset_taint to raise ledger_corrupted exception

test_cases.description: format_output raises an error or handles gracefully when passed output_format='auto', which violates the precondition
test_cases.input_description: Call format_output with output_format='auto'
test_cases.expected_error: Precondition violation: output_format must not be 'auto'
assertions: Function either raises an error or the contract invariant is otherwise enforced

test_cases.description: BlastRadiusResponse affected_nodes are strictly ordered by depth ascending, verified with nodes at depths 1, 3, 2 returned unordered from engine
test_cases.input_description: Blast radius engine mock returns nodes in non-depth order; CLI should present them depth-ascending
assertions: affected_nodes depths are in non-decreasing order

test_cases.description: TrustShowResponse history entries are ordered oldest-first, verified with entries having distinct timestamps
test_cases.input_description: Trust engine returns history with multiple entries; verify ordering
assertions: History timestamps are in chronological (ascending) order
